title: Colony-stimulating factor-2 triggers NNMT-dependent myofibroblast activation in head and neck cancer
task: Characterize CSF2-FOS-NNMT axis in myofibroblast activation independent of TGF-β signaling
key_results: Tumor-derived CSF2 correlates with CAF NNMT overexpression; CSF2 enhances myoCAF properties in NNMT-dependent manner; CSF2 induces FOS phosphorylation at Ser32 for nuclear translocation and NNMT transcription; CSF2 blockade overcomes TGF-β inhibitor resistance
comments: Novel TGF-β-independent stromal activation pathway identified with therapeutic potential; NNMT enzymatic role in myoCAF phenotype could be further characterized.

hypotheses.hypothesis: CSF2 drives myoCAF activation via FOS-mediated NNMT transcription independent of TGF-β
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; CSF2→FOS phosphorylation→NNMT transcription; TGF-β-independent; CSF2 blockade overcomes TGF-β inhibitor resistance

title: CAPRIN1-mediated sequestration of NCOA4 mRNA into stress granules drives sorafenib resistance in hepatocellular carcinoma
task: Identify CAPRIN1-NCOA4 stress granule axis in sorafenib resistance and ferroptosis suppression in HCC
key_results: High SG activity correlates with sorafenib resistance and poor survival; CAPRIN1 recruits NCOA4 mRNA into SGs via RGG domain to suppress NCOA4 translation and blunt ferroptosis; CAPRIN1 disruption restores NCOA4 and resensitizes resistant tumors to sorafenib
comments: Mechanistic insight into ferroptosis evasion through translational repression is novel; clinical validation in patient samples would strengthen therapeutic potential.

hypotheses.hypothesis: CAPRIN1-mediated NCOA4 mRNA sequestration in SGs suppresses ferroptosis and drives sorafenib resistance
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; CAPRIN1 interacts with NCOA4 mRNA via RGG domain; disruption restores NCOA4 and resensitizes to sorafenib

title: In vitro models of cell competition: current approaches and future directions
task: Review molecular mechanisms and in vitro model systems for studying cell competition in development and cancer
key_results: Cell competition functions as quality control mechanism in development and regeneration; dysregulation implicates role in cancer progression; classical 2D co-culture, organoid, and organ-on-chip platforms enable mechanistic investigation
comments: Comprehensive review of concepts and model systems but limited new quantitative experimental data or unified mechanistic framework across systems.

title: ZNRF1 deficiency disrupts Fas ligand trafficking and immune balance
task: Characterize ZNRF1-mediated FasL trafficking and immune checkpoint function in macrophages
key_results: Myeloid Znrf1 deletion causes age-dependent splenomegaly and elevated CD4+/CD8+ ratios upon allosensitization; ZNRF1 deficiency disrupts FasL terminal trafficking despite normal total FasL; ZNRF1 E3 activity strengthens Munc18-2–Syntaxin-3 interaction; Znrf1-deficient macrophages show impaired FasL-dependent T-cell killing
comments: Novel trafficking checkpoint identified with immune functional consequences; ZNRF1 catalytic requirement validates E3 mechanism.

hypotheses.hypothesis: ZNRF1 E3 activity couples lysosome-related organelle polarization to FasL fusion and surface exposure
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; ZNRF1 deficiency weakens Munc18-2–Stx3 interaction and impairs FasL docking/fusion

title: NUP93 facilitates the nuclear import of SOX2 to activate G3BP1 transcription and impairs gemcitabine response in pancreatic cancer
task: Identify NUP93-SOX2-G3BP1 axis in gemcitabine resistance in pancreatic cancer
key_results: NUP93 upregulation correlates with poor survival; NUP93 facilitates SOX2 nuclear import, which transcriptionally activates G3BP1; G3BP1 stabilizes RAD51 mRNA to promote homologous recombination repair and gemcitabine resistance
comments: Clear mechanistic axis identified with translational potential, though in vivo validation limited to xenograft models.

hypotheses.hypothesis: NUP93 promotes gemcitabine resistance by facilitating SOX2 nuclear import and G3BP1 activation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; NUP93 recognizes SOX2 NLS, facilitates import, activates G3BP1 transcription, and stabilizes RAD51

title: The Sirt2–Nur77 axis regulates muscle stem cell quiescence and senescence via epigenetic–metabolic synergy
task: Characterize the Sirt2-Nur77 axis in age-related muscle stem cell dysfunction
key_results: Sirt2 increases with age while Nur77 decreases; Sirt2 deacetylates Nur77 at K310 to promote degradation and weaken Myf5 activity; Sirt2 inhibition (AGK2) or Nur77 activation (CSNB) improved muscle function in aged mice
comments: K310R mutation phenotype validates the axis but mechanistic link between individual modifications and in vivo outcomes could be more precisely quantified.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Sirt2-mediated deacetylation of Nur77 K310 promotes age-related muscle decline
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; K310R mutation prevents deacetylation and preserves muscle function

title: Deltex E3 ubiquitin ligase 2 potentiates STING-mediated type I interferon response by K63-linked ubiquitination
task: Elucidate DTX2-mediated K63-ubiquitination of STING in type I interferon response
key_results: DTX2 promotes K63-linked ubiquitination of STING at K236 and K370, facilitating ER-to-Golgi translocation and downstream signaling; Dtx2−/− mice show impaired interferon production upon dsDNA/cGAMP stimulation and increased susceptibility to viral infection
comments: Mechanistic detail on ubiquitin chain topology is valuable but in vivo functional validation limited to viral infection models.

hypotheses.hypothesis: DTX2 potentiates STING signaling through K63-linked ubiquitination
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; DTX2 catalyzes K63 chains at K236/K370 to enable ER-to-Golgi translocation

title: HOXA9 orchestrates EMT and metastasis in oral cancer via transcriptional activation of vimentin and β-catenin signaling
task: Elucidate HOXA9-mediated EMT and metastasis in oral cancer via vimentin and Wnt signaling
key_results: HOXA9 upregulation correlates with lymph node-positive stage and higher grade; HOXA9 knockdown impairs proliferation/migration/invasion and reduces tumor volume and lung metastasis; HOXA9 transcriptionally activates VIM and facilitates β-catenin translocation; HOXA9 promoter hypomethylation correlates with upregulation
comments: Comprehensive epigenetic and transcriptional mechanisms identified with strong in vivo validation; potential for biomarker development.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Epigenetically altered HOXA9 drives EMT and metastasis via VIM/Wnt-β-catenin signaling
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; HOXA9 hypomethylation increases expression; HOXA9 activates VIM transcription and β-catenin translocation

title: Everolimus suppresses glucose transporter 3 membrane trafficking to improve therapeutic efficacy of umbilical cord blood-derived mesenchymal stem cell transplantation in diabetic retinopathy
task: Enhance mesenchymal stem cell efficacy in diabetic retinopathy via mTOR inhibition and glucose transporter regulation
key_results: Everolimus inhibited GLUT3 membrane trafficking, reduced mtROS production, and improved UCB-MSC survival in high glucose; STZ-induced DR rat model showed improved retinal function with GLUT3 knockdown or everolimus-pretreated MSCs
comments: Mechanistic pathway well-characterized but clinical translation remains at preclinical stage.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Everolimus enhances MSC viability by suppressing GLUT3-mediated glucose uptake and mtROS
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; everolimus inhibited mTORC1→reduced cofilin phosphorylation→suppressed GLUT3 translocation→reduced mtROS

title: LMO7-mediated POLR2A degradation promotes cellular senescence through the MDM4/p53/p21 axis
task: Determine role of LMO7-mediated POLR2A degradation in cellular senescence
key_results: POLR2A expression declines in senescent cells and aging tissues; POLR2A depletion induces senescence via p53/p21 pathway; LMO7 E3 ligase ubiquitinates and degrades POLR2A under senescence; LMO7 depletion abolishes POLR2A ubiquitination in H₂O₂-induced senescence
comments: Clear regulatory mechanism identified with age-relevant implications but broader tissue-type specificity and therapeutic intervention strategies not fully explored.

hypotheses.hypothesis: LMO7-mediated POLR2A degradation drives cellular senescence through MDM4/p53/p21
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; POLR2A knockdown activates p53/p21; MDM4 is key p53 mediator; LMO7 ubiquitinates POLR2A; LMO7 depletion rescues

title: TUFM: a central regulator in mitochondrial quality control and beyond
task: Characterize TUFM as a mitochondrial quality control coordinator and cellular fate regulator
key_results: TUFM orchestrates mitochondrial biogenesis, dynamics, and mitophagy through post-translational modifications; hyperactivation promotes oncogenesis while deficiency exacerbates cell death and neurodegeneration
comments: Review synthesizing mechanistic insights but limited to conceptual framework without new quantitative experimental data presented.

title: A bridge-like lipid transfer protein is critical for generation of invasive stages in malaria parasites
task: Characterize PfVPS13L1 lipid transfer protein in malaria parasite schizogony and invasive stage formation
key_results: PfVPS13L1 is a bridge-like LTP that mediates bulk lipid transfer from ER to nascent inner membrane complex (IMC); PfVPS13L1 loss-of-function reduces IMC growth and generates smaller anucleated progeny, impairing schizogony
comments: Critical role in parasite development identified but quantitative analysis of lipid flux and in vivo validation in infected hosts could strengthen conclusions.

hypotheses.hypothesis: PfVPS13L1 mediates bulk lipid transfer from ER to IMC critical for schizogony
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; PfVPS13L1 loss-of-function impairs IMC growth and schizogony efficiency

title: CBP phosphorylation maintains intestinal homeostasis by supporting the stem cell niche through versican
task: Determine role of CBP phosphorylation in intestinal stem cell niche maintenance via versican
key_results: Impaired CBP phosphorylation in CBPAA mice alters CBP-p53 binding, reduces versican expression, and impairs IEC proliferation; deleting p53 or providing versican rescues phenotype; ulcerative colitis tissues show impaired CBP phosphorylation
comments: Clinical relevance demonstrated but therapeutic versican administration kinetics and dosing not fully characterized.

hypotheses.hypothesis: CBP phosphorylation maintains versican expression to support the intestinal stem cell niche
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; phosphorylation impairment reduces versican and stem cell function; p53 deletion or versican replenishment rescues

title: IL-22 induces Paneth cell metaplasia in the colonic epithelium of ulcerative colitis, promoting wound healing via REG3A
task: Investigate IL-22-driven Paneth cell metaplasia and REG3A-mediated wound healing in ulcerative colitis
key_results: IL-22 promotes PCM formation by suppressing Notch signaling and inducing REG3A; REG3A enhances proliferation and wound healing in organoids; Reg3aΔIEC mice show delayed healing in acute/chronic DSS colitis; REG3A administration accelerates healing in patient-derived organoids
comments: Therapeutic relevance to UC well-demonstrated but detailed mechanistic link between PCM formation and specific REG3A-mediated signaling pathways could be expanded.

hypotheses.hypothesis: IL-22-induced PCM formation promotes wound healing via REG3A in UC
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; IL-22 suppresses Notch, induces REG3A; REG3A enhances proliferation and healing; Reg3a deletion impairs healing

title: 7SK-enriched exosomes suppress the cancer phenotypes in human lung cancer cells: insights from 2D and microfluidic 3D in vitro models
task: Assess exosome-mediated 7SK lncRNA delivery for lung cancer suppression
key_results: Exo-7SK elevated 7SK levels, reduced viability and proliferation, promoted apoptosis, inhibited migration/invasion, and impaired spheroid formation in 2D and 3D microfluidic cultures
comments: Comprehensive in vitro models tested but mechanism of 7SK's transcriptional regulation and in vivo efficacy not fully elucidated.

hypotheses.hypothesis: 7SK lncRNA suppresses lung cancer phenotypes through exosome-mediated delivery
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; Exo-7SK reduced proliferation, promoted apoptosis, inhibited invasion, and impaired spheroid formation

title: Biological effects of 5G-modulated 700 MHz RF-EMF exposure on neuronal and glial cell models under isothermal conditions
task: Assess biological effects of 5G-modulated 700 MHz RF-EMF on central nervous system cells under isothermal conditions
key_results: No statistically significant differences in mitochondrial ROS, cell viability, or apoptosis in astrocytes or neuroblastoma cells at SAR 0.08 or 4 W/kg for 1 h or 24 h exposure; positive controls with hydrogen peroxide confirmed assay sensitivity
comments: Rigorous negative result demonstrating absence of acute/subacute effects but limited to in vitro models and short exposure windows.

model_io.model: Primary rat cortical astrocytes + TEM cells
Human SH-SY5Y neuroblastoma cells + TEM cells

hypotheses.hypothesis: 5G-modulated 700 MHz RF-EMF induces oxidative stress and apoptosis in neural cells
hypotheses.result: Not confirmed; no significant differences at 0.08 or 4 W/kg under isothermal conditions

title: GABARAPL1 is important for the activation of HRI during eIF2α phosphorylation-dependent stress response to sodium arsenite
task: Characterize GABARAPL1 role in HRI activation during arsenite-induced integrated stress response
key_results: GABARAPL1 deficiency reduces eIF2α phosphorylation and impairs stress granule formation upon sodium arsenite exposure; GABARAPL1 facilitates HSP90-HRI interaction required for HRI kinase activation
comments: Novel mechanistic role for autophagy protein in stress response identified but in vivo validation and downstream functional consequences could be more extensively characterized.

hypotheses.hypothesis: GABARAPL1 facilitates HSP90-HRI interaction to enable HRI activation during arsenite stress
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; GABARAPL1 absence disrupts HSP90-HRI complex and impairs eIF2α phosphorylation

title: Study on the mechanism of action of HuaZhuoKeLi in modulating LCN2-mediated cellular pyroptosis to ameliorate ulcerative colitis
task: Investigate HuaZhuoKeLi-mediated LCN2-dependent pyroptosis inhibition in ulcerative colitis
key_results: Bioinformatics identified LCN2 as key pyroptosis-related hub gene in UC; HZKL alleviated colonic inflammation and reduced epithelial ultrastructural damage in DSS-induced colitis; HZKL downregulated LCN2 and attenuated pyroptosis-related signaling; HZKL enhanced intestinal epithelial cell proliferation and migration
comments: Comprehensive bioinformatics and functional validation but LCN2's precise role in NLRP3 inflammasome activation could be more mechanistically detailed.

hypotheses.hypothesis: HZKL ameliorates UC by suppressing LCN2-associated pyroptosis
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; HZKL downregulated LCN2 and pyroptosis-related signaling, enhanced epithelial proliferation/migration

title: A cough sound-based deep learning algorithm for accessible prompt detection of chronic obstructive pulmonary disease with smartphones
task: COPD detection from smartphone cough sounds using deep learning
key_results: AUC 0.94 (external validation), 92% sensitivity, 86% specificity; >91% sensitivity across all COPD severity stages
comments: Strong clinical performance, but real-world deployment effectiveness depends on smartphone audio standardization and generalization to diverse acoustic environments.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Voluntary cough sounds contain sufficient discriminative features for COPD detection comparable to spirometry
hypotheses.result: Confirmed with 0.94 AUC and 92% sensitivity in external cohort

hypotheses.hypothesis: Performance is robust across COPD severity stages and non-COPD respiratory conditions
hypotheses.result: Confirmed with >91% sensitivity across GOLD 1-4 stages and maintained accuracy across competing conditions

title: Few-shot android malware classification with quantum-enhanced prototypical learning and drift detection
task: Few-shot Android malware classification with concept drift adaptation
key_results: 99.70% accuracy on CCCS-CIC-AndMal-2020 (15 families), 99.33% on KronoDroid; CatBoost reduces features by 99.46% and 94.07% respectively while maintaining performance
comments: Impressive few-shot learning results, but practical deployment requires validation on truly zero-day malware families outside training distribution.

model_io.model: CatBoost feature selector + Prototypical networks + Quantum-enhanced classifier

hypotheses.hypothesis: Aggressive feature selection via CatBoost preserves discriminative malware signatures
hypotheses.result: Confirmed with 99.46% dimensionality reduction and maintained 99.70% accuracy

hypotheses.hypothesis: Prototypical networks enable effective few-shot classification with 5 samples per class
hypotheses.result: Confirmed with state-of-the-art performance outperforming baselines by 0.70-9.70%

hypotheses.hypothesis: Concept drift detection maintains temporal stability across evolving malware landscape
hypotheses.result: Confirmed with maximum accuracy degradation of only 0.24% across periods

title: On the constant depth implementation of Pauli exponentials
task: Constant-depth implementation of Pauli exponentials under linear nearest-neighbor connectivity
key_results: Z⊗n exponentials decomposed to constant depth using O(n) ancillae; applies to arbitrary Pauli exponentials and stabilizer circuits
comments: Rigorous theoretical construction with applications to fault-tolerant quantum computing; no empirical gate count comparisons provided.

model_io.model: Circuit decomposition scheme with qubit recycling

hypotheses.hypothesis: Novel rewrite rules enable constant-depth decomposition with two-body interactions only
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via fixed-point correctness proofs

title: KinForm: kinetics-informed feature optimised representation models for enzyme kcat and KM prediction
task: Enzyme kinetic parameter prediction via optimized protein representations
key_results: KinForm outperforms baselines on kcat and KM prediction; improvements most pronounced in low sequence similarity bins; binding-site pooling, intermediate-layer selection, PCA, and oversampling all contribute
comments: Careful ablation validates each component; removing sequence overlap between folds provides realistic generalization but may underestimate generalization on related proteins.

model_io.model: KinForm (multi-model residue embeddings + weighted pooling + PCA + oversampling)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Per-residue binding-site probability pooling improves kinetic parameter prediction vs. mean pooling
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; gains from weighted pooling validated in ablation

hypotheses.hypothesis: Concatenating intermediate-layer embeddings from multiple ESMs improves low-similarity generalization
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; largest improvements in low sequence similarity bins

title: Explainable hybrid AI CAD framework for advanced prediction of steel surface defects
task: Steel surface defect detection and classification
key_results: Fusion YOLO achieves AP 83.8% on NEU-DET; hybrid CNN-ViT classification reaches 99.7% F1-score; generalization F1 94.8% on GC10-DET
comments: Two-stage separation of detection and classification is pragmatic; generalization test on different dataset strengthens claims.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Separating localization and classification outperforms single-stage detectors
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; state-of-the-art AP and F1-scores achieved

hypotheses.hypothesis: Ensemble CNN-ViT captures local texture and global dependencies better than individual models
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via 99.7% F1-score on classification stage

title: Improving road safety in smart cities using machine learning techniques
task: Road safety prediction in smart cities via machine learning
key_results: XGBoost and Random Forest achieve 74% accuracy (KSI dataset); Random Forest 99% after Grid Search + undersampling; association rules identify speeding, aggressive driving, pedestrian collisions as high-risk factors
comments: 99% accuracy after hyperparameter tuning is likely overfit; association rule mining is interpretable but comparison between Toronto/Pakistan lacks statistical rigor.

model_io.model: Random Forest, XGBoost, and other ML/DL models
inputs: traffic accident data (Toronto KSI + Rawalpindi RTA datasets)

outputs: rules linking factors to fatal/major injuries

hypotheses.hypothesis: XGBoost and Random Forest outperform other models on traffic accident severity prediction
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 74–99% accuracy depending on hyperparameter tuning and undersampling

hypotheses.hypothesis: Speeding, aggressive driving, and traffic control absence are primary risk factors
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via association rules in both datasets

title: Optimized environmental prediction in smart buildings using Dynamic Greylag Goose algorithm and deep learning
task: Environmental prediction in smart buildings via optimization and deep learning
key_results: DGGO-LSTM achieves MSE 0.00119 and NSE 0.98247; 17–37% MSE reduction vs. GWO-LSTM, GGO-LSTM, WOA-LSTM; execution time 145.32 s (42% faster than WOA-LSTM)
comments: Dynamic Greylag Goose Optimization for dual feature selection and hyperparameter tuning is thorough; NSE≈0.98 indicates excellent fit.

inputs: IoT sensor data (temperature, humidity, air quality, sound, light)

hypotheses.hypothesis: DGGO dual optimization (feature selection + hyperparameter tuning) improves LSTM prediction accuracy vs. single-optimization baselines
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; MSE 0.00119 with 17–37% reduction vs. other algorithms

hypotheses.hypothesis: DGGO-LSTM is computationally more efficient than WOA-LSTM
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 42% faster execution time (145.32 vs. 251 s)

title: UTR-DynaPro: a CNN–transformer multimodal language model for decoding 5′UTR regulatory mechanisms
task: 5′UTR translation efficiency prediction via multimodal CNN-Transformer
key_results: UTR-DynaPro achieves 3.3%, 2.2%, 2.4% improvements over SOTA on translation efficiency and expression prediction; identifies known and novel regulatory motifs
comments: Multimodal fusion of sequence embeddings with biophysical features is well-motivated; attention-based motif discovery is interpretable.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Parallel CNN-Transformer captures both local motifs and long-range dependencies
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 2–3% improvements over SOTA

hypotheses.hypothesis: Dynamic fusion gate effectively integrates multimodal biological features
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; consistent performance across cell types

title: An optimized EfficientNetB0 framework with CLAHE-based preprocessing for accurate multi-class chest X-ray classification
task: Multi-label chest X-ray classification
key_results: Macro-average AUC 0.906, recall 0.824; Pneumonia AUC 0.950, Cardiomegaly AUC 0.946
comments: CLAHE preprocessing and class balancing are well-motivated but the 'multi-label complexity' claim oversimplifies the actual dataset difficulty.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Transfer learning with strategic class balancing outperforms DenseNet121 and MobileNetV2
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; superior macro-AUC and recall reported

title: Numerical study on the performance of a forced ventilation system under hydrogen leakage in an underground hydrogen equipment room
task: Forced ventilation system performance under hydrogen leakage
key_results: Proposed simple model predicts volume-averaged H₂ concentration trend; correctly predicts times to reach 1 and 4 vol%
comments: 3D transient RANS validated against simple model; practical engineering focus but limited discussion of model assumptions.

inputs: H₂ leakage rate, vent configuration, fan count

hypotheses.hypothesis: Simple model effectively predicts volume-averaged H₂ concentration
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; matches CFD trends and critical concentration thresholds

title: Enhancing MTC device security in LTE networks for the fast and secure BC-based group handover authentication protocol
task: Blockchain-based group handover authentication for MTC devices in LTE networks
key_results: Chimp Optimization Algorithm (ChOA) selects optimal relay with minimal energy, distance, delay; blockchain ensures integrity and confidentiality; significant reduction in energy, delay, and distance validated
comments: Combines optimization (ChOA) with blockchain for security; simulation results reported but hardware validation or comparison with existing protocols lacking in abstract.

hypotheses.hypothesis: ChOA-optimized relay selection reduces energy consumption, delay, and distance
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; simulation demonstrates improvements in network lifetime and efficiency

title: Remaining useful life prediction method based on gated dilation causal convolution
task: Remaining useful life prediction for rolling bearings
key_results: Gated dilated causal convolution framework reduces vanishing gradients; multi-feature squeeze-excitation captures local and global features
comments: Ablation studies validate component contributions, but absolute prediction errors (MAE, RMSE) not quantified in abstract.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Gated dilated causal convolution better models global and local features than standard CNNs
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via comparative and ablation studies

title: An embedded deep learning framework for real-time violence detection and alert generation
task: Real-time violence detection from video surveillance
key_results: Average accuracy 96.9%, F1 0.963, ROC–AUC 0.972 on five benchmarks + custom six-class dataset; 26 FPS and 38.4 ms/frame on Raspberry Pi 5; outperforms I3D, LRCN, ViT
comments: Lightweight CNN-GRU architecture with quantization and TensorRT optimization is practical; Grad-CAM explainability validates localization of violent regions.

model_io.model: Lightweight CNN (spatial feature extraction) + GRU (temporal modeling)

hypotheses.hypothesis: CNN-GRU with quantization/pruning achieves real-time throughput (26 FPS) on edge devices while maintaining >96% accuracy
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 38.4 ms/frame latency on Raspberry Pi 5

hypotheses.hypothesis: Spatio-temporal CNN-GRU modeling outperforms pure spatial (I3D) or transformer architectures for embedded deployment
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; superior efficiency and accuracy (ROC-AUC 0.972)

title: Artificial neural network analysis of a fractional cyber-epidemic model in wireless sensors under the proportional Hadamard–Caputo operator
task: Fractional cyber-epidemic modeling in wireless sensor networks
key_results: Proportional Hadamard–Caputo operator enables memory-dependent malware spread model; existence, uniqueness, and Ulam–Hyers stability proven; predictor-corrector scheme validated
comments: Rigorous mathematical framework with fixed-point theorems; simulations show stronger memory slows decay but quantitative epidemic metrics not discussed.

model_io.model: Fractional order SEIQRV malware model (6 device states)
inputs: fractional order α, memory kernel parameters

hypotheses.hypothesis: Fractional operators capture memory effects in malware propagation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; lower fractional order extends spread duration

hypotheses.hypothesis: Ulam–Hyers stability ensures robustness to modeling errors
hypotheses.result: Proven mathematically; small perturbations lead to small solution changes

title: Mathematical modeling of adaptive information security strategies using composite behavior models
task: Adaptive information security strategies via composite behavior models
key_results: Improvements in threat detection, mitigation speed, and resource optimization; superior scalability vs. existing methods
comments: Combines game theory and system dynamics but lacks concrete numerical comparisons with baselines in abstract.

model_io.model: Composite behavior model from game theory and system dynamics

hypotheses.hypothesis: Unified dynamic model captures aggregate behavior better than isolated models
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; improved threat detection and mitigation speed reported

title: Exploring the importance of shape on dynamic recognition of self-face or friend-face
task: Self-face vs. friend-face recognition from dynamic clips
key_results: Self-face motion identification depends critically on face shape; friend-face identification independent of observed shape
comments: Well-designed Deepfake manipulation of shape and motion; small sample sizes not disclosed; perceptual finding is psychologically novel.

inputs: video clips of self and friend faces

hypotheses.hypothesis: Self-face motion identification is shape-dependent; friend-face is shape-independent
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; self-faces show distinct perceptual characteristics

title: RGB-conditioned frequency domain refinement for sparse-to-dense depth completion
task: Depth completion from sparse measurements with RGB guidance
key_results: State-of-the-art performance with sharp edges and suppressed texture artifacts; frequency-domain decomposition decouples structure from texture transfer
comments: The frequency-domain approach is elegant but evaluation metrics (e.g., RMSE, MAE) are not quantitatively reported in the abstract.

hypotheses.hypothesis: RGB features should condition filtering rather than directly fuse with depth
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; reduces texture copying and edge blurring

hypotheses.hypothesis: Content-adaptive kernels preserve boundaries better than uniform filtering
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via wavelet sub-band decomposition

title: Confidence-calibrated federated graph attention for internet of things agents under latency SLOs
task: Federated graph attention for IoT agents under latency SLOs
key_results: ROC-AUC/PR-AUC ≈ 0.97–0.98; p99 latency 27.5 ms (enforcement) vs. 39.2 ms baseline; parameter-efficient updates ≤1.1 MB/round; SLO compliance <100 ms
comments: End-to-end latency decomposition and hardware validation (Raspberry Pi, Jetson, Intel NUC) are rigorous; security overhead (CKKS+SMPC) quantified in energy/latency.

inputs: IoT/IoMT sensor readings from N=100 clients
outputs: calibrated threat predictions for SDN enforcement

hypotheses.hypothesis: Federated graph-attention with trust-weighted aggregation improves calibration and satisfies p99 latency SLOs
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; low ECE and p99=27.5 ms on edge devices

hypotheses.hypothesis: PEFT (LoRA) updates reduce communication overhead without sacrificing discrimination
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; ≤1.1 MB/round with ROC-AUC ≈0.97

title: IoT enabled offshore infrastructure management and its impact on monitoring maintenance safety compliance and structural resilience
task: IoT impact on offshore infrastructure management
key_results: IoT implementation improves monitoring visibility and safety compliance; resilience linked to early fault detection rather than direct diagnostics
comments: Survey-based study without quantitative performance metrics; causal claims may be overstated from observational data.

hypotheses.hypothesis: IoT monitoring improves predictive maintenance and safety compliance
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via questionnaire analysis

hypotheses.hypothesis: Resilience improvements derive from proactive maintenance, not structural diagnostics
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; early fault detection identified as primary driver

title: Evaluating multimodal commercial and open-source large language models for dynamical astronomy: a benchmark study of resonant behavior classification
task: Multimodal LLM classification of mean-motion and secular resonances from astronomical images
key_results: Commercial LLMs reach F1=100% on simple cases, up to 94% on three-class ambiguous dataset; open-source models achieve F1≈90–96% on full binary benchmark without fine-tuning
comments: Zero-shot performance without training is impressive; most errors occur in transient/sticking regimes, indicating dataset difficulty.

model_io.model: Multimodal commercial LLMs (flagship models) + open-source models (large and small)
inputs: images of resonant arguments (RB-TEST, RB-PILOT, RB-SMALL, RB-FULL datasets)

hypotheses.hypothesis: LLMs classify resonances without training or fine-tuning at near-classical accuracy
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; F1=100% on unambiguous, ~90% on complex cases

hypotheses.hypothesis: Small open-source models achieve practically useful accuracy compared to commercial models
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; F1≈90–96% vs. commercial performance

title: Rigorous construction and classification of solitary-waves and exact soliton configurations in the nonlinear coupled Maccari system
task: Soliton and traveling-wave solutions of nonlinear coupled Maccari system
key_results: Generalized exponential rational function (GERF) method derives non-topological/topological solitons, kink-type and periodic singular waves; PDEs reduced to ODEs via wave-variable transformations
comments: Systematic analytical framework for exact solutions; applications to deep-water waves, optics, and plasma physics noted but not elaborated.

hypotheses.hypothesis: GERF method systematically constructs multiple classes of exact solutions to Maccabi PDEs
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; non-topological, topological, exponential, kink, and periodic solutions derived

title: A three-level discretization framework for dynamic behaviors of graphene-coated rotational blades with preset-angle sandwich structure under complex loads
task: Dynamic response analysis of graphene-coated rotational turbine blades under pulse loads
key_results: Chebyshev-Ritz-Galerkin three-level discretization avoids FEM convergence issues; synergistic effects of GPL weight fraction and rotational speed characterized
comments: Novel discretization method and comprehensive parametric study; however, validation against experimental data not mentioned in abstract.

inputs: graphene coating properties (GPL weight fraction, L/t ratio)
outputs: blade dynamic response, displacement, stress fields

hypotheses.hypothesis: Graphene reinforcement and rotation speed have synergistic effects on vibration suppression
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via parametric study; design guidelines provided

title: Topology optimization of wheel spoke cavities for lightweight design under bending fatigue and impact load cases
task: Multi-load-case topology optimization for lightweight aluminum alloy wheels
key_results: Peak von Mises stress reduced 19.25% (bending fatigue) and 14.57% (13° impact); mass reduced 0.53%; manufacturing constraints (10 mm min member, draw directions) preserved
comments: Compromise programming method rigorously calibrates weighting coefficients; industrially viable but modest mass reduction suggests aggressive design targets.

model_io.model: Region-constrained topology optimization (strain energy ratio weighting)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Depth-gradient cavities balance stress reduction and manufacturability better than empirical designs
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 19.25% fatigue stress reduction with 10 mm minimum member size

title: A structure-preserving diffusion-based zero-shot learning framework for multimodal magnetic flux leakage signal analysis
task: Zero-shot MFL signal analysis for pipeline defect detection via diffusion and cross-modal fusion
key_results: Structure-preserving diffusion improves SNR from 12.3 to 24.1 dB; macro F1 0.93 on known defects; zero-shot accuracy 0.84 on unseen defects (H-Mean 0.88)
comments: Innovative multimodal fusion (MFL, UT, IR) and zero-shot generalization are strong; cross-material/pipeline validation confirms practical utility.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Triple-constraint diffusion (gradient, morphology, frequency) preserves defect structure
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; SNR improvement from 12.3 to 24.1 dB

hypotheses.hypothesis: Zero-shot learning via semantic embedding transfers knowledge to unseen defect classes
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 0.84 accuracy and 0.88 H-Mean on test set

title: Predictive patterning via solid-state dewetting of transferred single-crystal films
task: Predictive patterning of single-crystal film dewetting for functional structures
key_results: Templated solid-state dewetting of Pd(100) on SiO₂ guides formation of regular patterns with <100> and <011> facets; anisotropic patterns controllable via crystallographic alignment and ambient
comments: Multiscale modeling successfully predicts anisotropic dewetting; demonstration with submicron-channel transistor electrodes validates functional applicability.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Surface energy and gas adsorption anisotropy determine facet preference and dewetting morphology
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: <011> facets favored with oxygen or high hydrogen flow; patterns reproducible via guided dewetting

title: The role of the Helmholtz potential on electrocatalytic activity
task: Predicting electrocatalytic activity via Helmholtz potential
key_results: Physical limit for hydrogen evolution reaction exchange current density is 10 A cm⁻²; thin semiconductor overlayers (1–10 nm) enhance activity through Helmholtz potential optimization
comments: Introduces Helmholtz potential as a descriptor beyond the Sabatier principle, though experimental validation is limited to literature data.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Electrochemical reaction kinetics depend on both Sabatier principle and Helmholtz potential at electrode surface
hypotheses.result: Validated against experimental hydrogen evolution reaction data; explains enhancement from semiconductor overlayers

title: Critical spin fluctuations across the superconducting dome in La2−xSrxCuO4
task: Understanding strange metal behavior via spin fluctuations in cuprates
key_results: Critical spin fluctuations show dynamical scaling across La₂₋ₓSrₓCuO₄ phase diagram; Griffiths phase extends over wide doping range consistent with disordered spin density wave quantum critical point
comments: Neutron scattering combined with theory provides compelling evidence, though the spatial disorder mechanism remains somewhat phenomenological.

model_io.model: Spin density wave in disordered metal (theory)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Strange metal behavior originates from critical spin fluctuations near a spin density wave quantum phase transition
hypotheses.result: Confirmed by temperature-dependent scaling and consistency between neutron data and disordered metal theory

title: Prediction of ambient-pressure high-temperature superconductivity in electronically modified transition-metal hydrides
task: Predicting high-Tc ambient-pressure superconductivity in carrier-doped transition-metal hydrides
key_results: MgAlFeH₆ predicted Tc ≈ 130 K; average projected DOS (geometric mean of total and H-projected DOS) correlates with Tc stronger than either component alone
comments: Carrier doping of intrinsically non-metallic compounds offers new discovery route; tradeoff between Tc and dynamic stability highlights materials design constraint.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Average projected DOS is superior descriptor of Tc in doped metal hydrides compared to total or H-projected DOS alone
hypotheses.result: Confirmed by stronger correlation; MgAlFeH₆ identified as high-Tc candidate

title: Electron-phonon physics at the exascale: a hybrid MPI-GPU-OpenMP framework for scalable Wannier interpolation
task: GPU-accelerated Wannier interpolation for electron-phonon coupling at exascale
key_results: Hybrid MPI-GPU-OpenMP implementation achieves 29-fold speedup vs. MPI-only EPW v5.9; scales nearly ideally to thousands of GPU nodes; enables 20 nm stanene nanoribbons previously intractable
comments: Excellent engineering achievement with demonstrated portability across NVIDIA, AMD, Intel accelerators; exascale readiness proven on Aurora and other leadership supercomputers.

hypotheses.hypothesis: GPU acceleration of Wannier interpolation with hybrid MPI-GPU-OpenMP achieves near-ideal scaling to thousands of nodes
hypotheses.result: Confirmed on Vista, Perlmutter, Aurora; 29× speedup relative to MPI-only baseline

title: Efficient foam-based thermal interface material functionalized with MWCNTs for CPU cooling applications: thermal performance modeling and Experimental studies
task: Thermal interface material design for CPU cooling via PVF/MWCNT foam composites
key_results: PVF/MWCNT (4 wt%, 2 mm, square geometry) achieves minimum CPU temperature 66.72°C under 80 W load; square shape outperforms circular due to better conformity; stable to 200°C with minimal weight loss
comments: Integrated thermal modeling and experimental validation; square geometry optimization is practical but modest improvement over circular design.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Square TIM geometry maximizes contact area and minimizes thermal resistance vs. circular design
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: square 4wt% PVF/MWCNT (2 mm) yields lowest CPU temperature at given heat load

title: A low-complexity compact dual-polarized patch antenna with high isolation and low cross-polarization for in-band full-duplex applications
task: Compact dual-polarized patch antenna for full-duplex wireless
key_results: Isolation >38 dB, cross-polarization <−40 dB, gain 4.3 dBi, size 0.18λ × 0.18λ × 0.03λ at 5.6 GHz
comments: Capacitive loading elegantly achieves both size reduction and cross-polarization suppression; performance metrics are competitive but incremental.

model_io.model: Microstrip patch antenna with capacitive loading

hypotheses.hypothesis: Capacitive loading simultaneously reduces size and suppresses cross-polarization
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via fabricated prototype with 0.18λ footprint and <−40 dB cross-polarization

title: Quad-band SIW antenna with micro-pocket enabled frequency-agile design for 5G/6G IoT applications
task: Quad-band frequency-agile SIW antenna for 5G/6G IoT
key_results: Four bands at 3.29, 4.47, 5.85, 7.07 GHz tunable to 3.29–8.4 GHz via micro-pockets; isolation >32 dB, FTBR >17.83 dB, gain 7.6 dBi, size 0.09 λg²
comments: Micro-pocket tuning is novel but requires material substitution; all ports co-polarized single-polarization is claimed as first-in-class.

model_io.model: SIW cavity with four resonating patches and micro-pockets

hypotheses.hypothesis: Micro-pockets enable wide frequency tunability (3.29–8.4 GHz) with simultaneous co-polarization
hypotheses.result: Confirmed in prototype; maintains >32 dB isolation across tuning range

title: Finite integration time can shift optimal sensitivity away from criticality
task: Optimal sensitivity under finite integration time constraints
key_results: Optimal operating point shifts away from criticality when integration time is finite; sensitivity depends on available time window rather than maximum theoretical value
comments: Important practical insight for neural information processing and sensor design, though the specific shift distance depends sensitively on system parameters.

model_io.model: Recurrent neural network near phase transition

hypotheses.hypothesis: Maximum sensitivity occurs at criticality only in the limit of infinite integration time
hypotheses.result: Confirmed analytically and computationally; optimal regime shifts with finite τ
